title: Wire the frontend to the backend
scenario: The `backend` Deployment is running (3 pods with label `app: backend`), but there's no Service in front of it. The frontend pods can't reach it by DNS. Create a ClusterIP Service named `backend-svc` that routes port 80 to the backend pods.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: backend-svc
  namespace: default
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: wrong-label
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80

objectives.description: Service routes to at least 1 healthy backend pod

hints.text: The starter YAML has `selector.app: wrong-label`. The backend pods have label `app: backend`.
Change `selector.app` from `wrong-label` to `backend`. That's the only fix needed.
Once fixed, the Cluster View will draw an edge from the Service to the backend pods.

solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: backend-svc
  namespace: default
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: backend
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
